intro: Migrating C# to Go simplifies architecture by switching from a feature-rich, enterprise runtime framework (.NET) to a minimal, concurrency-first systems language designed by Google. Developers trade verbose object hierarchies, inheritance, and rich language features for extreme compilation speeds, explicit error checking, and lightweight goroutines. This change emphasizes compositional simplicity and minimal resource footprints over syntactic abstractions.

diffs.from: Static, nominal system featuring a unified object hierarchy, classical class inheritance, and explicit interface implementations.
diffs.to: Static, structural system rejecting class inheritance in favor of composition, structural interfaces, and strict basic type rules.

diffs.from: Brace-scoped, verbose, object-oriented syntax with property shortcuts, annotations (attributes), and extensive LINQ methods.
diffs.to: Minimalist syntax eliminating semicolons, requiring explicit block formats, and lacking inheritance keywords or annotations.

diffs.from: Managed via the .NET generational garbage collector, separating stack allocations from optimized heap object pools.
diffs.to: Managed via a highly optimized, low-latency, concurrent tri-color mark-and-sweep garbage collector utilizing escape analysis.

diffs.from: Asynchronous task allocation utilizing async/await keywords backed by thread-pool scheduled Task workers.
diffs.to: Concurrency built directly into the language runtime using lightweight Goroutines, synchronized via Channels and Select blocks.

diffs.from: Deep base class hierarchy providing comprehensive web, database, cryptography, and enterprise platform integration.
diffs.to: Lean, high-performance library focused on networking, web services, microservice architecture, and basic structural utilities.

examples.title: Interface Structuring
examples.description: Contrasts a C# explicit interface implementation with Go's structural (implicit) interface system.

examples.title: Concurrent Execution Channel Pipeline
examples.description: Compares C# async parallel processing with a native Go channel and goroutine synchronization routine.

tips.title: Embrace Structural Interfaces
tips.body: In Go, structs do not explicitly declare that they implement an interface. If a struct implements all methods defined in an interface, it satisfies the interface implicitly, making decoupling natural.

tips.title: Handle Errors as Values
tips.body: Go does not use exceptions (`try/catch`). Functions return an error interface as their final return value. Check errors explicitly after every operation using idiomatic `if err != nil` code blocks.

tips.title: Favor Composition Over Inheritance
tips.body: Go has no `extends` keyword. Replicate C# class inheritance hierarchies by embedding structs within structs, allowing child types to transparently inherit inner structural methods.

tips.title: Transition Tasks to Goroutines
tips.body: Replace expensive C# Task pipelines with low-overhead Go channels and goroutines. Goroutines use dynamic, segmented stacks starting at 2KB, allowing millions of concurrent execution steps.
